```{r}
  # ◄ This pushes the labels further outward away from the arrow tips/points
  ggrepel.options = list(point.padding = 0.8, box.padding = 0.5, max.overlaps = Inf),
  alpha.var = 0.35,
  geom.ind = "point",
  col.var = "gray20",
  labelsize = 4
) + geom_text(
  data = custom_labels, 
  aes(x = x, y = y, label = Variable), 
  color = "gray20", 
  size = 4,
  fontface = "plain",
  inherit.aes = FALSE   # Prevents it from trying to map to the groups
) +
  scale_color_manual(values = AGE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = rep("transparent", length(unique(traits_NiN_age$age_class)))) + 
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 16, 16)) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA Biplot - Plant Traits",
    color = "Age class",
    x = "PC1 (40.1%)",
    y = "PC2 (23.2%)"
  ) +
  guides(fill = "none", shape = "none") 

biplot_age
```

```{r}
#| label: Individuals only plots, to see if age class occupies different trait space

fviz_pca_ind(
  pca_prcomp,
  habillage = traits_NiN_age$age_class,
  addEllipses = TRUE,
  repel = TRUE
)+
  scale_color_manual(values = AGE)


#Trait only, to see which traits drive the PCA
fviz_pca_var(
  pca_prcomp,
  repel = TRUE
)
```

This model is not logical as NiN and age class are categorical variables. Need to figure out which model could be more suitable to answer research question.

```{r}
#| label: glm model with NiN type as resonse variable to the traits?


#testtraits <- lmerTest::lmer(calluna_NiN_type ~ SLA_scaled * LDMC_scaled * height_cm_scaled * leaf_thickness_mean_scaled * leaf_area_scaled  + (1|plant_id), data=cleaned_data, na.action = na.exclude)

```

# Presenting results wet mass

```{r}
#| label: Plots to visualize wet mass emmeans output and dataframes for other plots


emmip(emmeans_lme4_wet_mass, calluna_NiN_type ~ age_class, CIs=TRUE) #If confidence intervals are largely overlapping it is more likely the groups are not siginficantly different
plot(emmeans_lme4_wet_mass, comparisons=TRUE) #These red arrows are specially calculated so that any overlap means "not significant" and no overlap means "significant."



#dataframe with multicomp, adding significance groups from emmeans
cld_wet_mass <- cld(
  emmeans_lme4_wet_mass,
  adjust = "tukey",
  Letters = letters
)

multicomp_wet_mass_df <- left_join(
  df_sum_wet_mass,
  cld_wet_mass %>%
    dplyr::select(age_class, calluna_NiN_type, .group),
  by = c("age_class", "calluna_NiN_type")
)


#data frame for estimate values from emmeans with all interactions
pairs(emmeans_lme4_wet_mass)
contrasts_wet_mass_df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emmeans_lme4_wet_mass))
head(contrasts_wet_mass_df)

contrasts_wet_mass_df <- contrasts_wet_mass_df |>
  separate(
    contrast,
    into = c("calluna_NiN_type", "age_class"),
    sep = " - "
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: point plot with error bars for wet mass 

point_plot_wet_mass <- ggplot(
  multicomp_wet_mass_df,
  aes(
    x = age_class,
    y = mean_wet_mass,
    colour = calluna_NiN_type,
    group = calluna_NiN_type
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_wet_mass - se_wet_mass,
      ymax = mean_wet_mass + se_wet_mass
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_wet_mass + se_wet_mass,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean wet mass (g) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Wet mass (g)", fill="NiN type")

point_plot_wet_mass

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("point_plot_wet_mass.png", plot = point_plot_wet_mass, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: Barchart wet mass

ggplot(multicomp_wet_mass_df, 
       aes(x = age_class, y = mean_wet_mass, group= calluna_NiN_type, fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           ymin = mean_wet_mass - se_wet_mass, ymax = mean_wet_mass + se_wet_mass)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width=0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_wet_mass + se_wet_mass,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean wet mass (g) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Wet mass (g)", fill="NiN type")
```

```{r}
#| label: Plot with circles and different levels of significance wet mass
#showing the size of the estimated differences between the age class and NiN type, a bit to noisy for presenting results

ggplot(contrasts_wet_mass_df,
       aes(calluna_NiN_type, age_class)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = abs(estimate),
        fill = estimate),
    shape = 21
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )),
    size = 5,
    nudge_y = 0.2)+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )
  )+
  labs(title="Mean wet mass (g) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type")+
  coord_equal()+
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "brown",
    mid = "white",
    high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint=0
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: beeswarm/ quasirandom plots for wet mass raw data with overlay of statistical tests


#Place letters above the tallest datapoint in each age class


cld_wet_mass <- cld_wet_mass %>%
  mutate(
    label_y = case_when(
      age_class == "Pioneer"  ~ 0.0075,
      age_class == "Building" ~ 0.0075,
      age_class == "Mature"   ~ 0.0075
    )
  )


ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = age_class, y = wet_mass, color=calluna_NiN_type)) +
   geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                        alpha = 0.5,
                        size = 1.3,
                        width = 0.15)+
  geom_violin(
    aes(fill = calluna_NiN_type),
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),
    alpha = 0.1,
    trim = TRUE,
    scale = "width",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
geom_pointrange(data=cld_wet_mass, 
                 aes(x=age_class, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=calluna_NiN_type), 
                color="black",
                size=0.8,
                fatten=2,
                position=position_dodge(width=1))+
   geom_text(
     data = cld_wet_mass,
     aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
     position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
     vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
     color = "black",
     fontface= "bold",
     show.legend = FALSE
  )+
   scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
   labs(x="Age class", y="Wet mass (g)", title="Mean leaf wet mass (g) of C. vulgaris across age class and NiN types", color="NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )


```

```{r}
#| label: Raincloud plot with half violins, jittered points and EMMs wet mass



ggplot(cleaned_data,
       aes(x = age_class,
           y = wet_mass,
           fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           colour = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  
  # Half violin ("cloud")
  stat_halfeye(
    adjust = 0.5,
    width = 0.4,
    justification = -0.25,   # moves violin left
    point_colour = NA,
    alpha = 0.15,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
  ) +
  
  # Raw points ("rain")
  geom_quasirandom(
    dodge.width = 0.8,
    width = 0.12,
    alpha = 0.4,
    size = 1
  ) +
  
  # EMMs
  geom_pointrange(
    data = cld_wet_mass,
    aes(
      x = age_class,
      y = emmean,
      ymin = lower.CL,
      ymax = upper.CL,
      group = calluna_NiN_type
    ),
    colour = "black",
    size = 0.7,
    fatten = 2,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # CLD letters
  geom_text(
    data = cld_wet_mass,
    aes(
      x = age_class,
      y = label_y,
      label = trimws(.group),
      group = calluna_NiN_type
    ),
    colour = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = NIN) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Age class",
    y = "Wet mass (g)",
    title = "Mean leaf wet mass (g) of C. vulgaris across age class and NiN types",
    colour = "NiN type",
    fill = "NiN type"
  ) +
  
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )+
  guides(fill = "none")
```

Best visuals so far, easy overview, less noise

```{r}
#| label: Without violins and boxplots wet mass

#Removed cld letter display

results_ggp_wet_mass <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = calluna_NiN_type, y = wet_mass, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.6,
                   size = 1,
                   width = 0.3)+
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_wet_mass, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=age_class), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
#  geom_text(
 #   data = cld_wet_mass,
  #  aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
   # position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    #vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    #color = "black",
    #fontface= "bold",
    #show.legend = FALSE
 # )+
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x=NULL, y="Wet mass (g)", title="Leaf wet mass (g) of" ~ italic("Calluna vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN types", color="NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.justification = "center"
  ) +
facet_wrap(~ age_class)

results_ggp_wet_mass

results_export_wet_mass <- results_ggp_wet_mass +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("results_export_wet_mass.png", plot = results_export_wet_mass, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}

#| label: Violin + boxplot wet mass

ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = age_class, y = wet_mass, color=calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.5,
                   size = 1,
                   width = 0.15)+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.35,
               width = 0.5,
               position = position_dodge(width=1))+
  geom_violin(
    aes(fill = calluna_NiN_type),
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),
    alpha = 0.15,
    trim = TRUE,
    scale = "width"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_wet_mass, 
                  aes(x=age_class, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=calluna_NiN_type), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
  geom_text(
    data = cld_wet_mass,
    aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    color = "black",
    fontface= "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x="Age class", y="Wet mass (g)", title="Mean leaf wet mass (g) of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN types", color="NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )
```

Good visuals, the violin is a nice visual to see the spread of the points, but it looks a bit messy with boxplot and violin together, the boxplot gives more information

```{r}
#| label: With Boxplot, removing pointrange


#Making a reusable plot function

ggp1 <- function(response, cld_data, ylab, title) {

  p <- ggplot(
    cleaned_data,
    aes(
      x = age_class,
      y = .data[[response]],
      color = calluna_NiN_type
    )
  ) +
      geom_quasirandom(
        dodge.width = 1,
        alpha = 0.5,
        size = 1,
        width = 0.1
      )+
    geom_boxplot(
      aes(
        fill = calluna_NiN_type,
        group = interaction(age_class, calluna_NiN_type)
      ),
      color = "black",
      alpha = 0.35,
      width = 0.5,
      position = position_dodge(width = 1)
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = cld_data,
      aes(
        y = label_y,
        label = trimws(.group),
        group = calluna_NiN_type
      ),
      position = position_dodge(width = 1),
      vjust = -0.5,
      color = "black",
      fontface = "bold",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
    labs(x = "Age class", y = ylab, title = title, color = "NiN type") +
    scale_y_continuous(
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
    ) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
}


ggp_wet_mass <- ggp1(
  "wet_mass",
  cld_wet_mass,
  "Mean leaf wet mass (g)",
  "Mean leaf wet mass (g) of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN types"
)

ggp_wet_mass

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("ggp_wet_mass.png", plot = ggp_wet_mass, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: violin and boxplot without pointrange

ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = age_class, y = wet_mass, color=calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.35,
                   size = 1.8,
                   width = 0.15)+
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.35,
               width = 0.5,
               position = position_dodge(width=1))+
  geom_violin(
    aes(fill = calluna_NiN_type),
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),
    alpha = 0.15,
    trim = TRUE,
    scale = "width"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = cld_wet_mass,
    aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    color = "black",
    fontface= "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+
scale_color_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
scale_fill_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
  labs(x="Age class", y="Wet mass (g)", title="Mean leaf wet mass (g) of C. vulgaris across age class and NiN types", color="NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: Another boxplot


ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = age_class, y = wet_mass, color=calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.6,
                   size = 1.8,
                   width = 0.15)+
  geom_boxplot(aes(group= interaction(age_class, calluna_NiN_type)),
               alpha = 0.35,
               width = 0.5,
               position = position_dodge(width=1)
  )+
  geom_text(
    data = cld_wet_mass,
    aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    color = "black",
    fontface= "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x="Age class", y="Wet mass (g)", title="Mean leaf wet mass (g) of C. vulgaris across age class and NiN types", color="NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

```

This plot is the most informative, but might be too redundant. I'll stick to the simpler plot without boxplot and significance lines

```{r}
#| label: Boxplot with raw datapoints, significance lines and letters wet mass

library(ggsignif)


# 1. Finn maks-verdien for wet_mass for å styre høyden på brakettene automatisk
max_y <- max(cleaned_data$wet_mass, na.rm = TRUE)

# 2. Data frame with manual star mapping based on your p-values
signif_wet_mass <- data.frame(
  xmin = c(
    2 - 0.33, # Building: BH
    2,        # Building: FH
    1,        # FH: Pioneer to Mature
    1 + 0.33, # TH: Pioneer to Building
    2 + 0.33  # TH: Building to Mature
  ),
  xmax = c(
    2 + 0.33, # Building: TH
    2 + 0.33, # Building: TH
    3,        # FH: Mature
    2 + 0.33, # Building: TH
    3 + 0.33  # Mature: TH
  ),
  # Height tiers to stack them neatly above the letters
  y_position = c(
    max_y * 1.30, 
    max_y * 1.45, 
    max_y * 1.60, 
    max_y * 1.75, 
    max_y * 1.90  
  ),
  # UPDATED: P-values mapped directly to star ratings
  annotation = c(
    "***", # p = 0.0005 (is < 0.001)
    "***", # p = 0.0002 (is < 0.001)
    "*",   # p = 0.0109 (is < 0.05)
    "***", # p = 0.0003 (is < 0.001)
    "***"  # p = 0.0007 (is < 0.001)
  ),
  group = 1:5 
)

# 3. Build the plot
ggp2_wet_mass <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = age_class, y = wet_mass, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(
    dodge.width = 1,
    alpha = 0.5,
    size = 1,
    width = 0.1
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    aes(
      fill = calluna_NiN_type,
      group = interaction(age_class, calluna_NiN_type)
    ),
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.40,
    width = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 1)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = cld_wet_mass,
    aes(
      y = label_y,
      label = trimws(.group),
      group = calluna_NiN_type
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),
    vjust = -0.5,
    color = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Signif Bracket Layer
  ggsignif::geom_signif(
    data = signif_wet_mass,
    aes(
      xmin = xmin, 
      xmax = xmax, 
      y_position = y_position, 
      annotations = annotation,
      group = group
    ),
    manual = TRUE,
    inherit.aes = FALSE, 
    color = "black",
    tip_length = 0.02,
    # Increased text size slightly since stars are smaller than text string expressions
    textsize = 4.2, 
    vjust = 0.4 # Centers stars vertically right on top of the bracket line
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
  labs(
    x = "Age class", 
    y = "Mean leaf wet mass (g)", 
    title = expression("Mean leaf wet mass (g) of " ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ " across age class and NiN types"), 
    color = "NiN type"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.10))
  ) +
  theme_classic() + 
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 1.5, xend = 1.5,
        y = 0, yend = 0.0072),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.3
  )+
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 2.5, xend = 2.5,
        y = 0, yend = 0.0072),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.3
  )

ggp2_wet_mass

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("ggp2_wet_mass.png", plot = ggp2_wet_mass, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)

```

# Presenting results height

Comparing results of emmeans post-hoc test, for visualizing the magnitude and direction of differences between group means

#A heatmap of estimates does not show: variability within groups, outliers, sample distributions,overlap among observations.

#Negative values → the first group has a lower mean than the second.

#Positive values → the first group has a higher mean than the second.

#Values near 0 → the groups are similar.

```{r}
#| label: Plots to visualize height emmeans output and dataframes for other plots


emmip(emmeans_height_cm, calluna_NiN_type ~ age_class, CIs=TRUE) #If confidence intervals are largely overlapping it is more likely the groups are not siginficantly different
plot(emmeans_height_cm, comparisons=TRUE) #These red arrows are specially calculated so that any overlap means "not significant" and no overlap means "significant."

#dataframe with multicomp, adding significance groups from emmeans
cld_height <- cld(
  emmeans_height_cm,
  adjust = "tukey",
  Letters = letters
)

multicomp_height_df <- left_join(
  df_sum_height,
  cld_height %>%
    dplyr::select(age_class, calluna_NiN_type, .group),
  by = c("age_class", "calluna_NiN_type")
)


#data frame for estimate values from emmeans with all interactions
contrasts_height_df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emmeans_height_cm))
head(contrasts_height_df)

contrasts_height_df <- contrasts_height_df |>
  separate(
    contrast,
    into = c("calluna_NiN_type", "age_class"),
    sep = " - "
  )




```

```{r}
#| label: point plot for height with error bars 

point_plot_height <- ggplot(
  multicomp_height_df,
  aes(
    x = calluna_NiN_type,
    y = mean_height_cm,
    colour = calluna_NiN_type
  )
) +
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_height, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=calluna_NiN_type),
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN, guide = "none") +
  geom_text(data = cld_height,
    aes(
      y = upper.CL + 2,
      label = .group,
      group = calluna_NiN_type
    ),
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),
    vjust = 0,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Vegetative height (cm) of"  ~ italic("Calluna vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type",
          x="NiN type", y="Height (cm)", colour = "NiN type") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()) +
  facet_wrap(~age_class)

point_plot_height
  
# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("point_plot_height.png", plot = point_plot_height, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

Not so easy to read as it looks like y-axis is counting the height

```{r}
#| label: Barchart height summary

ggplot(multicomp_height_df, 
       aes(x = age_class, y = mean_height_cm, group= calluna_NiN_type, fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           ymin = mean_height_cm - se_height_cm, ymax = mean_height_cm + se_height_cm)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width=0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_height_cm + se_height_cm,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean vegetative height (cm) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
  x="Age class", y="Height (cm)", fill="NiN type")
```

```{r}
#| label: Plot with circles and different levels of significance for height

# showing the size of the estimated differences between the age class and NiN type, a bit to noisy for presenting results

ggplot(contrasts_height_df,
       aes(calluna_NiN_type, age_class)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = abs(estimate),
        fill = estimate),
    shape = 21
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )),
    size = 5,
    nudge_y = 0.2)+
  xlab("NIN type")+
  ylab("Age class")+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )
  )+
  labs(title="Mean height (cm) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type")+
  coord_equal()+
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "brown",
    mid = "white",
    high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint=0
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: ggp1 plot height

#Place letters above the tallest datapoint in each age class

cld_height <- cld_height %>%
  mutate(
    label_y = case_when(
      age_class == "Pioneer"  ~ 54,
      age_class == "Building" ~ 54,
      age_class == "Mature"   ~ 54
    )
  )

ggp_height <- ggplot(
    cleaned_data,
    aes(
      x = age_class,
      y = height_cm,
      color = calluna_NiN_type,
      group = calluna_NiN_type,
      fill = calluna_NiN_type,
    )
  ) +
  stat_unique(
  geom = "point",
  position = position_dodge(width = 1),
  alpha = 0.8, 
  size = 1.8
) +
    geom_boxplot(
      aes(
        group = interaction(age_class, calluna_NiN_type)
      ),
      color = "black",
      alpha = 0.35,
      width = 0.5,
      position = position_dodge(width = 1)
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = cld_height,
      aes(
        y = label_y,
        label = trimws(.group),
        group = calluna_NiN_type
      ),
      position = position_dodge(width = 1),
      vjust = -0.5,
      color = "black",
      fontface = "bold",
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = NIN, name = "NiN type") +
    labs(x = "Age class", y = "Vegetative height (cm)", title = "Vegetative height (cm) of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type", color = "NiN type") +
    scale_y_continuous(
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
    ) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )


ggp_height

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("ggp_height.png", plot = ggp_height, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
  

```

For the report:

```{r}
#| label: Quasirandom + pointrange + EMM results plot height


results_ggp_height <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = calluna_NiN_type, y = height_cm, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  stat_unique(
    geom = "point",
    position = position_dodge(width = 1),
    alpha = 0.4, 
    size = 1.8
  ) +
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_height, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=age_class), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
  #geom_text(
   # data = cld_height,
    #aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
    #position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    #vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    #color = "black",
    #fontface= "bold",
    #show.legend = FALSE
 # )+
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x = NULL, y="Vegetative height (cm)", title="Vegetative height (cm) of" ~ italic("Calluna vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN types", color="NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.justification = "center"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class)

results_ggp_height

results_export_height <- results_ggp_height +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())


# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("results_export_height.png", plot = results_export_height, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

# Presenting results dry mass

```{r}
#| label: Plots to visualize dry mass emmeans output and data frames for other plots


emmip(emmeans_lme4_dry_mass, calluna_NiN_type ~ age_class, CIs=TRUE) #If confidence intervals are largely overlapping it is more likely the groups are not siginficantly different
plot(emmeans_lme4_dry_mass, comparisons=TRUE) #Overlapping arrows mean the groups are likely not significantly different from each other

#dataframe with multicomp, adding significance groups from emmeans
cld_dry_mass <- cld(
  emmeans_lme4_dry_mass,
  adjust = "tukey",
  Letters = letters
)

multicomp_dry_mass_df <- left_join(
  df_sum_dry_mass,
  cld_dry_mass %>%
    dplyr::select(age_class, calluna_NiN_type, .group),
  by = c("age_class", "calluna_NiN_type")
)


#data frame for estimate values from emmeans with all interactions
pairs(emmeans_lme4_dry_mass)
contrasts_dry_mass_df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emmeans_lme4_dry_mass))
head(contrasts_dry_mass_df)

contrasts_dry_mass_df <- contrasts_dry_mass_df |>
  separate(
    contrast,
    into = c("calluna_NiN_type", "age_class"),
    sep = " - "
  )



```

```{r}
#| label: point plot with error bars dry mass

point_plot_dry_mass <- ggplot(
  multicomp_dry_mass_df,
  aes(
    x = age_class,
    y = mean_dry_mass,
    colour = calluna_NiN_type,
    group = calluna_NiN_type
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_dry_mass - se_dry_mass,
      ymax = mean_dry_mass + se_dry_mass
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_dry_mass + se_dry_mass,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean dry mass (g) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Dry mass (g)", fill="NiN type")

point_plot_dry_mass

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("point_plot_dry_mass.png", plot = point_plot_dry_mass, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label:  Barchart dry mass
ggplot(multicomp_dry_mass_df, 
       aes(x = age_class, y = mean_dry_mass, group= calluna_NiN_type, fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           ymin = mean_dry_mass - se_dry_mass, ymax = mean_dry_mass + se_dry_mass)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width=0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_dry_mass + se_dry_mass,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean dry mass (g) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Dry mass (g)", fill="NiN type")
```

```{r}
#| label: Plot with circles and different levels of significance dry mass

ggplot(contrasts_dry_mass_df,
       aes(calluna_NiN_type, age_class)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = abs(estimate),
        fill = estimate),
    shape = 21
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )),
    size = 5,
    nudge_y = 0.2)+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )) + 
  labs(title="Mean dry mass (g) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type")+
  theme(plot.title.position = "plot") +
  coord_equal()+
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "brown",
    mid = "white",
    high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint=0
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: ggp plot dry mass with automatic plot function

#Place letters above the tallest datapoint in each age class

cld_dry_mass <- cld_dry_mass %>%
  mutate(
    label_y = case_when(
      age_class == "Pioneer"  ~ 0.0035,
      age_class == "Building" ~ 0.0035,
      age_class == "Mature"   ~ 0.0035
    )
  )

ggp_dry_mass <- ggp1(
  "dry_mass",
  cld_dry_mass,
  "Mean leaf dry mass (g)",
  "Mean leaf dry mass (g) of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN types"
)+
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 1.5, xend = 1.5,
        y = 0, yend = 0.003),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.3
  )+
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 2.5, xend = 2.5,
        y = 0, yend = 0.003),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = "grey80",
    linewidth = 0.3
  )

ggp_dry_mass

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("ggp_dry_mass.png", plot = ggp_dry_mass, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

This is used in the report:

```{r}
#| label: Without violins and boxplots dry mass



results_ggp_dry_mass <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = calluna_NiN_type, y = dry_mass, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.6,
                   size = 1,
                   width = 0.3)+
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_dry_mass, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=age_class), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
 # geom_text(
  #  data = cld_dry_mass,
   # aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
    #position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    #vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    #color = "black",
    #fontface= "bold",
    #show.legend = FALSE
 # )+
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x=NULL, y="Dry mass (g)", title="Leaf dry mass (g) of" ~ italic("Calluna vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN types", color="NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.justification = "center"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class)

results_ggp_dry_mass

results_export_dry_mass <- results_ggp_dry_mass +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("results_export_dry_mass.png", plot = results_export_dry_mass, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

# Presenting results leaf area

```{r}
#| label: Plots to visualize leaf area emmeans output and data frames for other plots 

emmip(emmeans_lme4_leaf_area, calluna_NiN_type ~ age_class, CIs=TRUE) #If confidence intervals are largely overlapping it is more likely the groups are not siginficantly different
plot(emmeans_lme4_leaf_area, comparisons=TRUE) #Overlapping arrows mean the groups are likely not significantly different from each other

#dataframe with multicomp, adding significance groups from emmeans
cld_leaf_area <- cld(
  emmeans_lme4_leaf_area,
  adjust = "tukey",
  Letters = letters
)

multicomp_leaf_area_df <- left_join(
  df_sum_leaf_area,
  cld_leaf_area %>%
    dplyr::select(age_class, calluna_NiN_type, .group),
  by = c("age_class", "calluna_NiN_type")
)


#data frame for estimate values from emmeans with all interactions
pairs(emmeans_lme4_leaf_area)
contrasts_leaf_area_df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emmeans_lme4_leaf_area))
head(contrasts_leaf_area_df)

contrasts_leaf_area_df <- contrasts_leaf_area_df |>
  separate(
    contrast,
    into = c("calluna_NiN_type", "age_class"),
    sep = " - "
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: point plot with error bars leaf area

point_plot_leaf_area <- ggplot(
  multicomp_leaf_area_df,
  aes(
    x = age_class,
    y = mean_leaf_area,
    colour = calluna_NiN_type,
    group = calluna_NiN_type
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_leaf_area - se_leaf_area,
      ymax = mean_leaf_area + se_leaf_area
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_leaf_area + se_leaf_area,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean leaf area (mm^2) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Leaf area (mm^2)", fill="NiN type")

point_plot_leaf_area

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("point_plot_leaf_area.png", plot = point_plot_leaf_area, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: Barchart leaf area
ggplot(multicomp_leaf_area_df, 
       aes(x = age_class, y = mean_leaf_area, group= calluna_NiN_type, fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           ymin = mean_leaf_area - se_leaf_area, ymax = mean_leaf_area + se_leaf_area)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width=0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_leaf_area + se_leaf_area,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean leaf_area (mm^2) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Leaf area (mm^2)", fill="NiN type")
```

```{r}
#| label: Plot with circles and different levels of significance leaf area

ggplot(contrasts_leaf_area_df,
       aes(calluna_NiN_type, age_class)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = abs(estimate),
        fill = estimate),
    shape = 21
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )),
    size = 5,
    nudge_y = 0.2)+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )) + 
  labs(title="Mean leaf area (mm^2) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type")+
  theme(plot.title.position = "plot") +
  coord_equal()+
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "brown",
    mid = "white",
    high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint=0
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: Without violins and boxplots leaf area

#Place letters above the tallest datapoint in each age class

cld_leaf_area <- cld_leaf_area %>%
  mutate(
    label_y = case_when(
      age_class == "Pioneer"  ~ 0.20,
      age_class == "Building" ~ 0.20,
      age_class == "Mature"   ~ 0.20
    )
  )




results_ggp_leaf_area <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = calluna_NiN_type, y = leaf_area, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.6,
                   size = 1,
                   width = 0.3)+
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_leaf_area, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=age_class), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
 # geom_text(
  #  data = cld_leaf_area,
   # aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
    #position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    #vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    #color = "black",
    #fontface= "bold",
    #show.legend = FALSE
 # )+
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x= NULL, y = expression(Leaf ~ area ~ (cm^2)), color = "NiN type", title=expression("Leaf area" ~ (cm^2) ~ "of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type"))+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.justification = "center"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class)

results_ggp_leaf_area

results_export_leaf_area <- results_ggp_leaf_area +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("results_export_leaf_area.png", plot = results_export_leaf_area, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

# Presenting results leaf thickness

```{r}
#| label: Plots to visualize emmeans output leaf thickness


emmip(emmeans_lme4_leaf_thickness, calluna_NiN_type ~ age_class, CIs=TRUE) #If confidence intervals are largely overlapping it is more likely the groups are not siginficantly different
plot(emmeans_lme4_leaf_thickness, comparisons=TRUE) #Overlapping arrows mean the groups are likely not significantly different from each other


#dataframe with multicomp, adding significance groups from emmeans
cld_leaf_thickness <- cld(
  emmeans_lme4_leaf_thickness,
  adjust = "tukey",
  Letters = letters
)

multicomp_leaf_thickness_df <- left_join(
  df_sum_leaf_thickness,
  cld_leaf_thickness %>%
    dplyr::select(age_class, calluna_NiN_type, .group),
  by = c("age_class", "calluna_NiN_type")
)


#data frame for estimate values from emmeans with all interactions
pairs(emmeans_lme4_leaf_thickness)
contrasts_leaf_thickness_df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emmeans_lme4_leaf_thickness))
head(contrasts_leaf_thickness_df)

contrasts_leaf_thickness_df <- contrasts_leaf_thickness_df |>
  separate(
    contrast,
    into = c("calluna_NiN_type", "age_class"),
    sep = " - "
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: point plot with error bars leaf thickness

point_plot_leaf_thickness <- ggplot(
  multicomp_leaf_thickness_df,
  aes(
    x = age_class,
    y = mean_leaf_thickness,
    colour = calluna_NiN_type,
    group = calluna_NiN_type
  )
) +
  geom_line(
    colour = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.6,
    position=position_dodge(width = 0.9)
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_leaf_thickness - se_leaf_thickness,
      ymax = mean_leaf_thickness + se_leaf_thickness
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_leaf_thickness + se_leaf_thickness,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean leaf thickness (mm) of C. vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Leaf thickness (mm)", fill="NiN type")

point_plot_leaf_thickness


# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("point_plot_leaf_thickness.png", plot = point_plot_leaf_thickness, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: Barchart leaf thickness

ggplot(multicomp_leaf_thickness_df, 
       aes(x = age_class, y = mean_leaf_thickness, group= calluna_NiN_type, fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           ymin = mean_leaf_thickness - se_leaf_thickness, ymax = mean_leaf_thickness + se_leaf_thickness)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width=0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_leaf_thickness + se_leaf_thickness,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean leaf thickness (mm) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="Leaf thickness (mm)", fill="NiN type")
```

```{r}
#| label: Plot with circles and different levels of significance leaf thickness


ggplot(contrasts_leaf_thickness_df,
       aes(calluna_NiN_type, age_class)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = abs(estimate),
        fill = estimate),
    shape = 21
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )),
    size = 5,
    nudge_y = 0.2)+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )) + 
  labs(title="Mean leaf thickness (mm) of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type")+
  theme(plot.title.position = "plot") +
  coord_equal()+
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "brown",
    mid = "white",
    high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint=0
  ) 




```

```{r}
#| label: ggp plot leaf thickness with automatic plot function

#Place letters above the tallest datapoint in each age class

cld_leaf_thickness <- cld_leaf_thickness %>%
  mutate(
    label_y = case_when(
      age_class == "Pioneer"  ~ 0.83,
      age_class == "Building" ~ 0.83,
      age_class == "Mature"   ~ 0.83
    )
  )

ggp_leaf_thickness <- ggp1(
  "leaf_thickness_mean",
  cld_leaf_thickness,
  "Mean leaf thickness (mm)",
  expression("Mean leaf thickness (mm) of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type")
)

ggp_leaf_thickness

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("ggp_leaf_thickness.png", plot = ggp_leaf_thickness, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

This is in the report:

```{r}
#| label: Without violins and boxplots leaf thickness
#Removed cld from plot


results_ggp_leaf_thickness <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = calluna_NiN_type, y = leaf_thickness_mean, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.6,
                   size = 1,
                   width = 0.3)+
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_leaf_thickness, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=age_class), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
#  geom_text(
 #   data = cld_leaf_thickness,
  #  aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
   # position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    #vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    #color = "black",
    #fontface= "bold",
    #show.legend = FALSE
  #)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Leaf thickness (mm)", color = "NiN type", title = "Mean leaf thickness (mm) of" ~ italic("Calluna vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type")+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.7),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.justification = "center"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class)

results_ggp_leaf_thickness

results_export_leaf_thickness <- results_ggp_leaf_thickness +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("results_export_leaf_thickness.png", plot = results_export_leaf_thickness, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

# Presenting results SLA

```{r}
#| label: Plots to visualize emmeans output for SLA

emmip(emmeans_lme4_SLA, calluna_NiN_type ~ age_class, CIs=TRUE) #If confidence intervals are largely overlapping it is more likely the groups are not siginficantly different
plot(emmeans_lme4_SLA, comparisons=TRUE) #Overlapping arrows mean the groups are likely not significantly different from each other


#dataframe with multicomp, adding significance groups from emmeans
cld_SLA <- cld(
  emmeans_lme4_SLA,
  adjust = "tukey",
  Letters = letters
)

multicomp_SLA_df <- left_join(
  df_sum_SLA,
  cld_SLA %>%
    dplyr::select(age_class, calluna_NiN_type, .group),
  by = c("age_class", "calluna_NiN_type")
)


#data frame for estimate values from emmeans with all interactions
pairs(emmeans_lme4_SLA)
contrasts_SLA_df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emmeans_lme4_SLA))
head(contrasts_SLA_df)

contrasts_SLA_df <- contrasts_SLA_df |>
  separate(
    contrast,
    into = c("calluna_NiN_type", "age_class"),
    sep = " - "
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: point plot with error bars SLA

point_plot_SLA <- ggplot(
  multicomp_SLA_df,
  aes(
    x = age_class,
    y = mean_SLA,
    colour = calluna_NiN_type,
    group = calluna_NiN_type
  )
) +
  geom_line(
    colour = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.6,
    position=position_dodge(width = 0.9)
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_SLA - se_SLA,
      ymax = mean_SLA + se_SLA
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_SLA + se_SLA,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean SLA of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="SLA", fill="NiN type")
point_plot_SLA


# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("point_plot_SLA.png", plot = point_plot_SLA, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: Barchart SLA

ggplot(multicomp_SLA_df, 
       aes(x = age_class, y = mean_SLA, group= calluna_NiN_type, fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           ymin = mean_SLA - se_SLA, ymax = mean_SLA + se_SLA)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width=0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_SLA + se_SLA,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean SLA of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="SLA", fill="NiN type")



#| label: Plot with circles and different levels of significance SLA

ggplot(contrasts_SLA_df,
       aes(calluna_NiN_type, age_class)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = abs(estimate),
        fill = estimate),
    shape = 21
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )),
    size = 5,
    nudge_y = 0.2)+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )) + 
  labs(title="Mean SLA of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type")+
  theme(plot.title.position = "plot") +
  coord_equal()+
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "brown",
    mid = "white",
    high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint=0
  ) 




```

```{r}
#| label: ggp plot SLA with automatic plot function

#Place letters above the tallest datapoint in each age class

cld_SLA <- cld_SLA %>%
  mutate(
    label_y = case_when(
      age_class == "Pioneer"  ~ 118,
      age_class == "Building" ~ 118,
      age_class == "Mature"   ~ 118
    )
  )

ggp_SLA <- ggp1(
  "SLA",
  cld_SLA,
  expression(SLA ~ (mm^2 ~ g^"-1")),
  expression("Specific leaf area (SLA) of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type")
)

ggp_SLA

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("ggp_SLA.png", plot = ggp_SLA, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: Without violins and boxplots SLA



results_ggp_SLA <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = calluna_NiN_type, y = SLA, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.6,
                   size = 1,
                   width = 0.3)+
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_SLA, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=age_class), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
#  geom_text(
 #   data = cld_SLA,
  #  aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
   # position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    #vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    #color = "black",
    #fontface= "bold",
    #show.legend = FALSE
 # )+
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(SLA ~ (cm^2 ~ g^-1)), color = "NiN type", title = expression("Specific leaf area" ~ (cm^2 ~ g^-1) ~ "of" ~ italic("Calluna vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type"))+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.95),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.justification = "center"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class)

results_ggp_SLA

results_export_SLA <- results_ggp_SLA +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("results_export_SLA.png", plot = results_export_SLA, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

# Presenting results LDMC

```{r}
#| label: Plots to visualize emmeans output LDMC


emmip(emmeans_lme4_LDMC, calluna_NiN_type ~ age_class, CIs=TRUE) #If confidence intervals are largely overlapping it is more likely the groups are not siginficantly different
plot(emmeans_lme4_LDMC, comparisons=TRUE) #Overlapping arrows mean the groups are likely not significantly different from each other


#dataframe with multicomp, adding significance groups from emmeans
cld_LDMC <- cld(
  emmeans_lme4_LDMC,
  adjust = "tukey",
  Letters = letters
)

multicomp_LDMC_df <- left_join(
  df_sum_LDMC,
  cld_LDMC %>%
    dplyr::select(age_class, calluna_NiN_type, .group),
  by = c("age_class", "calluna_NiN_type")
)


#data frame for estimate values from emmeans with all interactions
pairs(emmeans_lme4_LDMC)
contrasts_LDMC_df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emmeans_lme4_LDMC))
head(contrasts_LDMC_df)

contrasts_LDMC_df <- contrasts_LDMC_df |>
  separate(
    contrast,
    into = c("calluna_NiN_type", "age_class"),
    sep = " - "
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: point plot with error bars LDMC

point_plot_LDMC <- ggplot(
  multicomp_LDMC_df,
  aes(
    x = age_class,
    y = mean_LDMC,
    colour = calluna_NiN_type,
    group = calluna_NiN_type
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_LDMC - se_LDMC,
      ymax = mean_LDMC + se_LDMC
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_LDMC + se_LDMC,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean LDMC of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="LDMC", fill="NiN type")
point_plot_LDMC

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("point_plot_LDMC.png", plot = point_plot_LDMC, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)


#| label: Barchart LDMC


ggplot(multicomp_LDMC_df, 
       aes(x = age_class, y = mean_LDMC, group= calluna_NiN_type, fill = calluna_NiN_type,
           ymin = mean_LDMC - se_LDMC, ymax = mean_LDMC + se_LDMC)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width=0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = mean_LDMC + se_LDMC,
      label = .group
    ),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    vjust=-0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  )+ labs(title="Mean LDMC of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type",
          x="Age class", y="LDMC", fill="NiN type")



#| label: Plot with circles and different levels of significance LDMC


ggplot(contrasts_LDMC_df,
       aes(calluna_NiN_type, age_class)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(size = abs(estimate),
        fill = estimate),
    shape = 21
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )),
    size = 5,
    nudge_y = 0.2)+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )) + 
  labs(title="Mean LDMC of Calluna vulgaris across age class and NiN type")+
  theme(plot.title.position = "plot") +
  coord_equal()+
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "brown",
    mid = "white",
    high = "darkgreen",
    midpoint=0
  ) 




```

```{r}
#| label: ggp plot LDMC with automatic plot function

#Place letters above the tallest datapoint in each age class

cld_LDMC <- cld_LDMC %>%
  mutate(
    label_y = case_when(
      age_class == "Pioneer"  ~ 0.52,
      age_class == "Building" ~ 0.52,
      age_class == "Mature"   ~ 0.52
    )
  )

ggp_LDMC <- ggp1(
  "LDMC",
  cld_LDMC,
  expression(LDMC ~ (g ~ g^"-1")),
  expression("Leaf dry matter content (LDMC) of" ~ italic("C. vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type")
)

ggp_LDMC

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("ggp_LDMC.png", plot = ggp_LDMC, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

In the report:

```{r}
#| label: Without violins and boxplots LDMC



results_ggp_LDMC <- ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(x = calluna_NiN_type, y = LDMC, color = calluna_NiN_type)) +
  geom_quasirandom(dodge.width = 1, 
                   alpha = 0.6,
                   size = 1,
                   width = 0.3)+
  geom_pointrange(data=cld_LDMC, 
                  aes(x=calluna_NiN_type, y=emmean, ymin=lower.CL, ymax=upper.CL, group=age_class), 
                  color="black",
                  size=0.8,
                  fatten=2,
                  position=position_dodge(width=1))+
#  geom_text(
 #   data = cld_LDMC,
  #  aes(y = label_y, label = trimws(.group), group = calluna_NiN_type),       
   # position = position_dodge(width = 1),   # Aligns letters with the pointranges
    #vjust = -0.5,                             # Nudges letters slightly upward
    #color = "black",
    #fontface= "bold",
    #show.legend = FALSE
 # )+
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(LDMC ~ (g ~ g^-1)), color = "NiN type", title = expression("Leaf dry matter content" ~ (g ~ g^-1) ~ "of" ~ italic("Calluna vulgaris") ~ "across age class and NiN type"))+
  scale_y_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))
  )+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.justification = "center"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class)

results_ggp_LDMC

results_export_LDMC <- results_ggp_LDMC +
  theme(plot.title = element_blank())

# Save with specific dimensions and high DPI
ggsave("results_export_LDMC.png", plot = results_export_LDMC, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#| label: SLA and LDMC relationship


ggplot(cleaned_data,
       aes(x = SLA, y = LDMC)) +
  
  # scatter points
  geom_point(aes(color = calluna_NiN_type),
             size = 1.5, alpha = 0.4) +
  
  # fitted regression line by age class
  geom_smooth(
    aes(colour = calluna_NiN_type),
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE,
    linewidth = 1.3,
    colour = "black"
  ) +
  
  # optional: label
  labs(
    x = "SLA",
    y = "LDMC",
    title = "SLA vs LDMC",
    color = "NiN type"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank()
  )


ggplot(cleaned_data, aes(SLA, LDMC)) +
  geom_point(
    colour = "grey70",
    alpha = 0.25,
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(colour = calluna_NiN_type),
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1.3
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ age_class) +
  scale_color_manual(values = NIN) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = NIN) 

```
